name: Brennercom
notes:
We have a selective peering policy and evaluate each request based on its merits. We also require from our peers:

* common sense
* must filter customer prefixes strictly
* must not leak prefixes from non-customer sessions (even if the prefix is from a customer)
* an updated peeringdb entry
* reasonable prefix aggregation
* responsive NOC
* peers IX-link must not be congested
* IPv6 peering, if peer AS is v6 enabled
* must not be our customer, or a customer of a existing peer
* do not send automated emails to our NOC for whatever reason; humans only!

Peering through route servers is usually preferred over dedicated BGP sessions, especially for low-volume, low-prefix peers that are already connected to the route servers. This is to avoid unnecessary maintenance and configuration overhead.

We filter all our customers with strict prefix-lists (along with other industry standard filtering and security measures), no exceptions.
